description: Mailbox delivery semantics (v0.4, §8.7, §13): at-least-once + explicit consume/ack + dedup + submitter-bound isolation. Behavioural guarantees a JSON Schema cannot check; proven against a conformant Hub + its consuming agent.
obligation: A human sends a directive to agent:X; it lands in X's durable mailbox. (1) At-least-once: X drains it, crashes before ack, and on the next drain (after the visibility window) the SAME directive `id` is redelivered with a FRESH `t`/`jti` (§9.7 per-delivery signing). (2) Dedup: X's consumer, seeing the same `id` twice, acts at most once (§13.4). (3) Consume: once X acks the `id`, the Hub MUST NOT redeliver it. (4) Isolation: a second agent Y (bearer scoped to agent.id=Y) draining `GET /v1/inbox` receives ONLY Y's mailbox — never X's directive — and an ack from Y referencing X's `id` is a no-op that reveals nothing about X's mailbox (§9.1 submitter-binding applied to the inbox). (5) Addressee check (§13.4): if X's directive is delivered to Y's consumer over the webhook channel (no Hub-side mailbox gate), Y MUST reject it — a VALID, in-window signature for agent:X is refused because `to` != Y, distinct from dp-006's tampered-`to` case. (6) jti replay (§9.7): replaying the exact same (directive, signature) bytes within the window is rejected by the agent's jti cache, independent of `id` dedup. (7) FIFO on first delivery; redelivery MAY reorder, so correctness relies on `id` dedup, not order. (8) Durability (§3.1): an unacked directive survives a Hub restart and stays drainable until acked or expired; note that agent-side id-dedup is at-most-once only within the agent's dedup horizon, so cross-restart at-most-once requires an idempotent side effect or a persisted dedup set (§13.4).
